info.title: Event Endpoint Management Admin API
info.description: The Admin API provides programmatic access to your Event Endpoint Management deployment and features from other systems.

servers.description: This is the base path for the Admin API URL on the server endpoint, which is also available in the Event Endpoint Management UI.
This is the base path for the Admin API URL on the admin endpoint.

etag.description: An identifier which can be supplied with the If-None-Match header to indicate that unchanged results should return 304 with no data.

ei-pagination-total.description: Total number of items available for retrieval (including current page).

entryId.name: entryId
entryId.description: The ID of the Catalog entry.

optionId.name: optionId
optionId.description: The ID of the option in the Event source.

page.name: page
page.description: The page number of results to retrieve. If this number exceeds the available data, then the last page is returned.

maxPageSize.name: maxPageSize
maxPageSize.description: The maximum number of entries to return for a given page. If not specified, all data items will be returned.

disableValidation.name: disableValidation
disableValidation.description: disable immediate validation against the remote server

eemAccessToken.description: To use the API, create an access token in the Event Endpoint Management UI.

certificates.description: Certificates or CAs to trust, required to connect to the server via SSL.

items.description: X509 certificate presented by an endpoint

sans.description: A list of subject alternative names of the certificate

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

lastUpdated.description: Time in ms since the epoch which represents the last time the cluster information was updated.

credentials.description: Credentials be used by the gateway to connect to Kafka

type.description: The type of credentials. Used by parsers to determine how to handle the other fields

data.description: Details of the SASL credential data to authenticate with Kafka

username.description: SASL username to authenticate with Kafka

password.description: SASL password to authenticate with Kafka

mechanism.description: SASL mechanism to authenticate with Kafka

region.description: Optional field containing the AWS MSK Region.

scope.description: Optional field containing the scope for requesting the token from the token endpoint.

audience.description: Optional field containing the audience for requesting the token from the token endpoint.

oauthTokenUri.description: Optional URI of the OAuth token endpoint

trustedCertificates.description: Certificates or CAs to trust, required to connect to OAuth server.

items.description: X509 certificate presented by an endpoint

sans.description: A list of subject alternative names of the certificate

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

clientCertificateAndKey.description: Client certificate and key to use in mTLS

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

groups.description: List of collaborators for this Cluster

id.description: The ID of your newly created Cluster

400.description: Bad input

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

409.description: A Cluster with the specified ID or name already exists

422.description: The specified Cluster could not be validated using Kafka discovery mechanisms e.g. connection fails, certs mis-matched, SASL mis-match

get.description: List all Clusters within an Organization. If a page range is specified, then this will return the requested range

bootstrapServers.description: Bootstrap servers for this cluster - only viewable with permissions

certificates.description: Certificates or CAs to trust, required to connect to the server via SSL.

items.description: X509 certificate presented by an endpoint

sans.description: A list of subject alternative names of the certificate

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

organization.description: Organization this cluster belongs to.

lastUpdated.description: Time in ms since the epoch which represents the last time the cluster information was updated.

credentials.description: Credentials be used by the gateway to connect to Kafka

type.description: The type of credentials. Used by parsers to determine how to handle the other fields

data.description: Details of the SASL credential data to authenticate with Kafka

username.description: SASL username to authenticate with Kafka

mechanism.description: SASL mechanism to authenticate with Kafka

region.description: Optional field containing the AWS MSK Region.

scope.description: Optional field containing the scope for requesting the token from the token endpoint.

audience.description: Optional field containing the audience for requesting the token from the token endpoint.

oauthTokenUri.description: Optional URI of the OAuth token endpoint

trustedCertificates.description: Certificates or CAs to trust, required to connect to OAuth server.

sans.description: A list of subject alternative names of the certificate

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

clientCertificateAndKey.description: Client certificate and key to use in mTLS

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

groups.description: List of collaborators for this Cluster

validation.description: Details of the visibility of this cluster amongst the gateways

status.description: Overall status of all of the gateways. | `pending` = Pending validation - we are waiting for gateways to report a status | `error` = No Gateways can see a Cluster/Topic - error state - either the configuration is incorrect, or there is a network issue | `success/partial` = success state - Some Gateways can see a Cluster/Topic | `success` = success state - All Gateways can see a Cluster/Topic

gatewayGroups.description: Gateway group names and if they can connect to this cluster

status.description: Status on whether this gateway group can see the associated cluster. | `pending` = Pending validation - we are waiting for gateways to report a status | `error` = error state - No Gateways can see the cluster | `success/partial` = success state - Some Gateways can see the cluster | `success` = success state - All Gateways can see the cluster

validationErrors.description: List of failed validation stages. If this array is empty, it can be assumed that this gateway group can connect and see the cluster.

items.description: `network` = we cannot resolve this address | `certificates/tls` = we do not trust this address | `securityConfig` = this endpoint does not support the configured type of security (MTLS, SASL Plain etc) | `credentials` = provided username/password not accepted | `notKafka` = server is resolvable but is not responding in the way a kafka server should | `unsupported` = version of current gateway does not support required capability

400.description: Invalid request, only one of page and maxPageSize were supplied or max page size exceeded.

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

put.description:
Update a Cluster using the 'id' as the identifier.

Note - You can view all of the Clusters (including their 'id') by using GET /clusters

delete.description:
Delete a Cluster.

All related Catalog Entries must be deleted prior to deleting a cluster. Note - You must be the owner to delete a Cluster. You can view all of the Clusters that you own (including their 'id') by using GET /clusters

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

409.description: catalog entries are still using this cluster and it cannot be deleted

post.description: Create a subscription to a Catalog Entry

entryId.description: The list of entry ids this subscription gives access to, currently only supports 1.

credentials.description: Credentials a client uses to connect to an Event Gateway

type.description: The type of credentials. Used by parsers to determine how to handle the other fields

data.description: Details of the SASL credential data to authenticate with an Event Gateway

username.description: SASL username to authenticate with an Event Gateway

password.description: SASL password to authenticate with an Event Gateway

mechanism.description: SASL mechanism to authenticate with Kafka

hashedPasswords.description: Hashed passwords from APIC to authenticate clients against an Event Gateway.

subjectCommonName.description: Subject Common Name to match on.

clientId.description: Optional client ID if this subscription is a legacy APIC one.

contact.description: Subscription contact details.

gateways.description: A list of gateway IDs that can access this data. If this property is missing or contains an empty list then this subscription has no gateway restrictions

id.description: The ID of your newly created Subscription

400.description: Bad input

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

409.description: Subscription with the specified ID already exists

get.description:
List all Subscriptions created by the current user in an Organization. If a page range is specified, then this will return the requested range.

Note - This will not include the subscription credentials

get.description: List all registered Event Gateways in an Organization. If a page range is specified, then this will return the requested range.

items.description: Detailed information about any registered gateways

organization.description: Organization this Event Gateway belongs to.

gatewayId.description: Unique Event Gateway ID within this group.

contact.description: The contact information for this Event Gateway

lastContact.description: Epoch time in ms of the last contact from this gateway.

endpoints.description: Endpoints that the gateway is listening on.

certificates.description: Certificates or CAs to trust, required to connect to the server via SSL.

items.description: X509 certificate presented by an endpoint

sans.description: A list of subject alternative names of the certificate

expiry.description: Expiration date of the certificate in epoch millis

features.description: Feature set in use by gateway

capabilities.description: Freeform Description of what capabilities the gateway has

get.description: List all Event Sources created by the current user in an Organization. If a page range is specified, then this will return the requested range.

organization.description: Organization this Event Source belongs to.

clusterId.description: ID for the cluster that contains the connection details for this Event Source.

encoding.description: The encoding of the data on this Event Source

contact.description: Who to contact for more information about this Event Source.

description.description: A description for this Event Source

name.description: Actual topic name in the cluster.

partitions.description: The number of partitions for this topic. Discovered from the Kafka cluster

replicas.description: The number of replicas for this topic. Discovered from the Kafka cluster

uuid.description: The uuid of the topic. Discovered from the Kafka cluster

type.description: Type of Schema from the list of valid types

description.description: Description of the data available on the Event Source

tags.description: List of tags associated with this Event Source

items.description: Capabilities enum.

validation.description: Details of the visibility of this event source amongst the gateway groups.

status.description: Status for all of the gateway groups. Can they see the topic associated with this event source? | `pending` = Pending validation - we are waiting for gateways to report a status | `error` = error state - No Gateway Groups can see the topic | `success/partial` = success state - Some Gateway Groups can see the topic | `success` = success state - All Gateway Groups can see the topic

gatewayGroups.description: Gateway group names and if they can connect to this cluster and find the associated topic.

status.description: Status on whether this gateway group can see the topic associated with this event source. | `pending` = Pending validation - we are waiting for gateways to report a status | `error` = error state - No Gateways can see the topic | `success/partial` = success state - Some Gateways can see the topic | `success` = success state - All Gateways can see the topic

validationErrors.description: List of failed validation stages. If this array is empty, it can be assumed that this gateway group can connect and see the topic.

items.description: `notFound` = topic not found in cluster.

groups.description: List of collaborators for this Event Source

contacts.description: List of contacts for this Event Source

delete.description:
Delete an Event Source

The Event Source must have no attached published or archived Options. Note - You must be the owner to delete an Event Source. You can view all of the Event Sources that you own (including their 'id') by using GET /eventsources

parameters.name: eventSourceId
parameters.description: The ID of the Event Source to delete.

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

404.description: This Event Source does not exist

409.description: Event Source could not be deleted due to a conflict

get.description: Read a list of Subscriptions to this Event Source

parameters.name: eventSourceId
parameters.description: The ID of the Event Source to read Subscriptions for.

contact.description: The contact details of the owner of this Subscription.

eventSource.description: Information about the Event Source this Subscription is for.

option.description: Information about the option this Subscription is for.

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

404.description: This Event Source does not exist

get.description: List all Options created by the current user in an Organization. If a page range is specified, then this will return the requested range. To view all options with 11.7.1 or later controls, you must specify the header 'Accept: application/vnd.ibm.eem.v2+json'. If you omit the header or specify 'application/json', then the response includes only options that can be represented with 11.7.0 or earlier controls.

organization.description: Organization this Option belongs to.

eventSourceId.description: Event source that this option belongs to

alias.description: Unique, descriptive alias for this option

description.description: Longer description used to describe what this option is used for

gatewayGroups.description: List of gateway groups this option should be published to

hidden.description: Indicates if this option is visible in the catalog

allowSubscriptions.description: Indicates if this option is allowing new subscriptions

lastUpdated.description: Unix Epoch timestamp of last update to approval request.

status.description: The status of this approval request.

description.description: Description of why the request was created.

contact.description: Contact details of the person making the request.

outcome.description: Description of the outcome of this request.

subscriptionId.description: The id of the subscription associated with this request.

applicationId.description: The id of the application associated with this request.

path.description: The JSON path specifying the field to redact. JSON path is currently restricted to just field names (no arrays or functions)

delete.description:
Delete an Option.

The Option must be unpublished with no remaining Subscriptions. Note - You must be the owner to delete an Option. You can view all of the Options that you own (including their 'id') by using GET /options

parameters.name: optionId
parameters.description: The ID of the Option to delete.

403.description: Access denied for this user and/or role

409.description: Option could not be deleted due to a conflict

get.description: List all Event Endpoints in an Organization. If a page range is specified, then this will return the requested range. To view all options with 11.7.1 or later controls in an Event Endpoint, you must specify the header 'Accept: application/vnd.ibm.eem.v2+json. parameters.description: Flag to indicate whether to include users that have no assets. True means return all defined users, whether they have assets or not. Default is false.

parameters.description: Given a searched field, filter the response to only include users who match in username or in their subject i.e topics, options etc.
